career_board.title: Career Evidence Sign
career_board.purpose: Map CodeForge work to real software-career skills -- each with the exact repo artifact that proves it, and the honest gaps.
career_board.market_note: Basis: BLS 2024 medians (Software Developers $133,080 - QA/Testers $102,610 - Technical Writers $91,670; ~317,700 CS/IT openings/yr through 2034) + O*NET work styles + current public postings (Roblox, Extreme Networks, Wyetech, Veeva, BioIntelliSense, PlayOn, Xsolla). CodeForge's strongest overlap: internal tools - release/build - DevOps/SRE - QA automation - technical docs.
career_board.status_legend: proven=current repo artifact | partial=some proof, needs more | missing=no proof | planned=idea only | needs_update=stale | human_review_required=uncertain

skills.skill: Explain a feature in plain English
skills.why_it_matters: Entry roles screen for clear communication and the ability to explain work (Roblox, Extreme early-career).
skills.next_proof_task: Record a 60-second narrated demo of one system.

skills.skill: Document what changed, why, and how to verify it
skills.why_it_matters: Employers want changes that are auditable, not just made.
skills.next_proof_task: Keep every CHANGELOG entry pairing the change with its verify command.

skills.why_it_matters: The core of every entry Python/tools role.
skills.next_proof_task: Keep modules small, typed, and single-responsibility.

skills.why_it_matters: Conventional Commits + branch->PR->merge signal process discipline.
skills.next_proof_task: Keep the issue -> PR -> merge loop visible (e.g. forge-audit #3 -> #4).

skills.why_it_matters: QA/test and entry-dev roles expect tests as a habit, not an afterthought.
skills.next_proof_task: Hold coverage at/above the stage threshold on the default branch.

skills.why_it_matters: O*NET lists attention to detail as a top work style for devs, QA, and writers.
skills.next_proof_task: Keep the classification registry validating (no orphans/dupes).

skills.skill: Complete a small feature end to end
skills.why_it_matters: Proof you can finish, not just start.
skills.next_proof_task: Keep each feature: filed, tested, documented, merged green.

skills.skill: Maintain a clean README (purpose - install - run - test)
skills.why_it_matters: The first thing an interviewer reads.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a demo GIF above the fold.

skills.why_it_matters: Technical-writer-adjacent signal that also helps every reviewer.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a short quickstart tutorial for a first-time player.

skills.why_it_matters: One-button, reproducible workflows read as reliability.
skills.next_proof_task: Keep the control panel consistent across all repos.

skills.why_it_matters: Entry hiring optimizes for learning velocity over experience.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a short spike-branch note documenting picking up one new tool/library.

skills.skill: Work from requirements and communicate them clearly
skills.why_it_matters: Turning a request into a plan is a core early-career signal.
skills.next_proof_task: Capture one requirement -> plan -> build trace as a short doc.

skills.why_it_matters: Automation-first thinking is the #1 repeated intermediate ask (BioIntelliSense, AppZen).
skills.next_proof_task: Keep before/after timing evidence for each automation win (e.g. ritual 45s -> 1s).

skills.skill: Build or improve a readiness ritual
skills.why_it_matters: Startup/shutdown discipline maps directly to release/DevOps work.
skills.next_proof_task: Add the deep ritual mode for pre-push/portfolio checks.

skills.why_it_matters: Debugging discipline is a top intermediate signal; a written case study proves method.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a second case study with a before/after metric.

skills.why_it_matters: Release/DevOps roles want CI ownership and coverage discipline.
skills.next_proof_task: Keep CI green-before-merge and coverage at threshold.

skills.why_it_matters: Internal-tools/platform roles are CodeForge's strongest market overlap.
skills.next_proof_task: Point to forge-audit (separate flagship) as the reusable-tool exemplar.

skills.why_it_matters: Release engineers own playbooks, gates, and rollback paths.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a rollback runbook to pair with the push-ready gate.

skills.why_it_matters: Docs-as-code is a repeated intermediate ask (Mujin, Veeva).
skills.next_proof_task: Add an API/tutorial doc for the engine tick.

skills.skill: Track project evidence and next actions
skills.why_it_matters: Project control read as ownership and follow-through.
skills.next_proof_task: Keep pm status computed from filed state, never hand-maintained.

skills.why_it_matters: Intermediate roles want risks called out, not hidden.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a decision memo that lists risks/tradeoffs for one design choice explicitly.

skills.why_it_matters: Keeping docs and code in lockstep is a maintenance-discipline signal.
skills.next_proof_task: Tighten the docs-impact check into a stricter per-file diff rule.

skills.skill: Create a reusable part with tests and reuse notes
skills.why_it_matters: A cataloged reusable component is concrete platform evidence.
skills.next_proof_task: Catalog more reusable parts as they mature, each with a reuse map.

skills.skill: Build a full-stack web surface (backend API + server-rendered frontend)
skills.why_it_matters: Full-stack roles want a Python backend plus a real browser surface; a recurring 2026 ask (see docs/research).
skills.next_proof_task: Add a decoupled React/TypeScript front end (separate flagship) consuming GET /api/status.

skills.skill: Model a relational database and evolve it with migrations
skills.why_it_matters: PostgreSQL + schema migrations is the single most-named production-data ask in 2026 postings.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a second migration from a real model change (e.g. a new column) with a downgrade path.

skills.why_it_matters: ADRs make architecture tradeoffs explicit -- a senior signal.
skills.next_proof_task: Add an ADR for the cast/seed architecture.

ownership.keel: I made the three architecture-law calls (canonical state, derive-don't-store, framework-free) and wrote each ADR to defend the tradeoff I accepted.

skills.why_it_matters: SRE/release roles want gates that prevent unsafe actions.
skills.next_proof_task: Keep the allowlisted runner (never raw shell) + push-ready gate documented.

skills.why_it_matters: Observability/diagnostics are the core of SRE.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a lightweight metrics/health snapshot to the ritual report.

skills.why_it_matters: Standards-setting is how seniors scale their impact.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a review rubric for incoming changes.

ownership.keel: I authored the repo's contributor standard (CONTRIBUTING.md) - the process every contributor, human or AI, must follow.

skills.skill: Maintain truth between docs and code
skills.why_it_matters: Trustworthy documentation is a repeated senior/SRE value (DexCare).
skills.next_proof_task: Broaden truth check to more drift-prone claims.

skills.skill: Connect engineering work to measurable outcomes
skills.why_it_matters: Seniors tie work to metrics (latency, toil, coverage).
skills.next_proof_task: Keep a metrics note per optimization (e.g. 40ms -> 1ms Nagle fix).

skills.skill: Design systems other engineers could use
skills.why_it_matters: Platform/lead roles shape how others work.
skills.next_proof_task: Publish codeforge-shelf to a package index (deferred: a distribution decision), or split Layer 1/2 physically.

skills.why_it_matters: Safe shipping/rollback is central to release/SRE roles.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a rollback runbook for the container/Docker deploy path too.

skills.why_it_matters: Teaching artifacts prove standards-setting and mentorship.
skills.next_proof_task: Flesh out one lesson end-to-end with a quiz and a worked example.

skills.skill: Create runbooks and postmortem templates (and fill one)
skills.why_it_matters: Operational discipline is the clearest SRE bridge.
skills.next_proof_task: Add a runbook for a real operation (e.g. the demo deploy rollback).

skills.skill: Build platform-style tooling used by multiple consumers
skills.why_it_matters: Platform engineering serves many teams/projects.
skills.next_proof_task: Publish the private repos' scorecards to an interviewer on request; add a second CI workflow to each so the fleet reads all-green.

skills.skill: Integrate an LLM behind a mockable, schema-enforced boundary
skills.why_it_matters: AI-assisted engineering is a 2026 differentiator; the hireable skill is a testable, schema-enforced boundary, not a raw API call.
skills.next_proof_task: Exercise the live path in a gated, keyed integration run and capture the evidence (the seam is proven; a real call is not yet run in CI).

skills.skill: Instrument a service with structured logs and metrics
skills.why_it_matters: Seniors are judged on operability; structured logs + a Prometheus /metrics endpoint are a recurring observability ask.
skills.next_proof_task: Add latency histogram buckets and scrape the endpoint from a Prometheus/Grafana demo, captured as evidence.

skills.skill: Translate scholarly research into a working, tested system
skills.why_it_matters: Turning a literature survey into a disciplined, evidence-labeled implementation is a senior differentiator (research-to-architecture), not just consuming papers.
skills.next_proof_task: Extend the lab with a third subject or a new evaluator dimension, still human-governed.

ownership.keel: I directed a research report into an evidence-labeled build: I set the scope, chose what to translate versus defer, and can defend why each label (evidence-backed, extrapolation, experimental, not-recommended) sits where it does.

skills.skill: Design an evaluator-guided search with human governance
skills.why_it_matters: Evolutionary/program-synthesis concepts plus AI governance and human-in-the-loop selection are 2026 differentiators; the hireable skill is a bounded, auditable, human-final design.
skills.next_proof_task: Add typed mutation operators (autonomous mutation) only after measurement justifies it, with Josh's approval.

ownership.keel: I designed the guided search to be bounded and human-final: hard gates before weighted objectives, no autonomous mutation in v1, and the human kept outside the loop for security-sensitive edits.

skills.skill: Build supply-chain security evidence: SBOM, dependency + license auditing
skills.why_it_matters: Supply-chain security (NIST SSDF, SBOMs, dependency and license auditing) is a 2026 hiring bar for platform and security-adjacent roles. CodeForge ships the evidence stack (CycloneDX SBOM via `make sbom`, pip-audit + Dependabot dependency auditing, CodeQL, detect-secrets); the forge-audit flagship extends it into a four-layer license-compliance evaluator (per-file SPDX scan, GPL/AGPL dependency scan, SBOM validation + a staleness cross-check), proven against real repos it did not build.
skills.next_proof_task: Surface forge-audit's four-layer license scorecard (SBOM validation + freshness) in the codeforge README Evaluation section.
